Resources – Tools, Part 2
2
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Executive Summary
• 	To continue off a point mentioned in Part 1: There is no one solution
that's going to work for everyone
• 	These tools just keep on getting better but there's definitely no perfect
tool. Everyone is always looking for a better solution
• 	Moving to different tools is not a really pleasant thing, but it is doable
Tools:
• 	Aweber (http://productlaunchformula.com/goaweber) - I love Aweber
so much that I had them create a private label for me called
Profollow.com. They are now identical services, there's no difference
between them. This is a tool that does one thing really well - it's where
you host your lists. It sends your emails out either as broadcasts or as
autoresponder messages. I've been using it for more than 10 years, it's a
great service
• 	MailChimp (http://productlaunchformula.com/mailchimp) - This is a
free list host/management service (similar to Aweber) that a lot of people
ask about. I'm not a big fan of MailChimp, but some people think it's
easier to use. They have a restrictive terms of service that doesn’t allow
you to send out affiliate promotions, so not a good long-term solution
• 	Other list hosts: (Convertkit, Maropost, iContact, Constant
Contact, Email Deliverability, and more…)A lot of the alternative
services have less restrictive terms of service agreements compared to
Aweber, in terms of importing existing lists. They can be good if you have
a big list that you've built somewhere and want to import. The downside
is lower deliverability.
• 	Infusionsoft (http://productlaunchformula.com/infusionsoft) - Very
similar to Ontraport with similar pricing. I just started using this in 2015.
I’d recommend comparing both, but these wouldn't be something you'd
want to get when you're first starting out
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 6 --

TRANSCRIPT
Resources – Tools, Part 2
3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
• 	Ontraport (http://productlaunchformula.com/ontraport) - This is a
full all-in-one solution. It does list management similar to Aweber, but it
also has a shopping cart and affiliate tracking/management built in. It's
an extremely powerful tool.
• 	1ShoppingCart (http://productlaunchformula.com/shoppingcart) -
This is similar to, but not as sophisticated as, Ontraport. It’s got
integrated list management, a shopping cart, and affiliate management.
It’s less expensive, much simpler, but not recommended for list hosting.
If I was going to use 1ShoppingCart, I would probably use it in tandem
with Aweber.
• 	To summarize: Aweber, huge fan. Ontraport, big fan. 1ShoppingCart, a
great place to start out. And Infusionsoft, another strong contender
there,
• 	Remember: “different horses for different courses”. Don’t overthink
this, just choose what works best for you.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Transcript
Okay, so now we're going to talk about email list management and
shopping carts.
A few ground rules here, remember horses for courses. There are different tools
that are good for different jobs, there's no one perfect solution. Just because
I'm using something or you're seeing someone else using something doesn't mean it's
the right solution for you. These tools do keep on getting better and better and there
is no perfect tool. Everyone's always looking for a better solution. These tools, they're
inherently, they're tough to make a shopping cart work perfect. They're tough to
make an email list host work perfect. The ground rules are always changing so the
people creating these tools, it's a tough road. Everyone's always looking for a better,
different solution, that's just the reality, and they do keep on getting better.
Moving solutions isn't easy, it isn't pleasant, but it is doable. It's not like if you
make a decision here it has to be once and for all forever.
They do all have their own tutorials, there's a lot of different tools. I can't
possibly compete with them and make tutorials. They keep their tutorials up to date.
Since the tools are always evolving, they're always adding new capabilities and new
features. They're always updating their tutorials so we don't have tutorials for them,
for these tools that we've built.
Some of these recommendations will have affiliate links. They're going to be
recommendations that I believe in. I'm never going to tell you to go use something
that I don't believe in because well it would be insane for me to do that because I
can't risk my relationship with you to make a few pennies or a few dollars with an
affiliate link.
In terms of email list management, big fan of AWeber. Used this
one for over 10 years. They've got great service and great deliverability. When you're
using a list host deliverability is the number one thing. That's just the fact that when
you send an email it will land in your subscriber's inboxes. If you're just starting out
in this business that might sound simple but it's not, it's complicated, it's very, very,
very complicated. That's really at the end of the day what you're paying for. AWeber's
really good with the deliverability and that's why it's a huge fan for years. Eventually,
things got too big and complicated in my business that we just needed some different
capabilities so after over 10 years I moved away from AWeber. You can get their
products on productlaunchformula.com/goaweber
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Resources – Tools, Part 2
5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
ConvertKit, this is a new one, at least a new one to me but I've been really
hearing great things about ConvertKit. Really, really like what they're doing, I'm
interested in what they're doing so it's definitely one you should check out at
productlaunchformula.com/convertkit.
MailChimp, lots of people start here, it's simple to use. It's free to use for us
to 1,000 subscribers which if you're just starting out that might seem like you'll never
go past 1,000 subscribers but, trust me, you will. It's free to start out with but it's not
free forever if you start actually building something. I'm not a huge fan of their terms
of service. They might have updated this but the last time I looked you couldn't
promote affiliate offers or joint venture offers which to me is just too big a restriction
to deal with. Like I said, a lot of people use it and it is free to start and it's easy to use
but I'm not the biggest fan.
There's lots and lots of others, especially as you get more sophisticated and
start to spend more money. Maropost is one we've used. iContact, Constant Contact,
Email Delivered, and there are a ton more, lots of these. Again, the number one thing
when you're picking one is deliverability, that is the number one thing you want with
it, with a list host.
Now, there are some where you actually get an integrated shopping cart and list
management and affiliate management.
Infusionsoft is one that's popular. It's completely integrated list
management, shopping cart, affiliate management. It's super powerful. This is what
we use. It's complicated, it's not the easiest thing to use whatsoever. This is one that
I use. This is a big decision for us to go from AWeber and sort of a bunch of different
solutions put together to move to Infusionsoft. It is expensive, it's complicated, but
it's working really, really well for us. It's productlaunchformula.com/infusionsoft. If
you're really building something that's going to scale this is one place you might want
to look.
Ontraport, very similar to Infusionsoft in power and features. Frankly, it
came down to trying to pick between the two of these for us and we did end up with
Infusionsoft. I do know a lot of people are using Ontraport at
productlaunchformula.com/ontraport. Yeah, these two are competitors with each
other. We ended up picking Infusionsoft but we know a lot of people with Ontraport
that are happy there as well.
Then another one that is sort of integrated. It's got the shopping cart, it's got
the list management, it's got the affiliate management built in but it's much cheaper,
not nearly as robust, is 1Shoppingcart. You just go to
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
